input: May sends no warning before she tears the music from the stand. Behold a balm from the Keys. A vision of childhood wonder. Now we've let slip the dogs of hesychasm, careening slowly through the hallway past cameras and lasers till every breath is a tipping swell, and each heartbeat cracks beneath the floor. A peaceable gondola floating over a sinking feeling. Inside I am small and confused, my true proportion. At this scale DNA rearranges the future, tiny antennae fold up in the Casimir space of love beyond the force of radio. Here I remember the cannonade of her seashell eyes, whirling beneath a seagull brow, a covering of light, flat white sand, pastels, burrowing crabs.
